# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"warning","summary":"User preferences model is email-only — must be updated for SMS channel support before SMS can be preference-gated","detail":"The UserPreferences model (src/models/user-preferences.ts) was designed with an "email is the only notification channel" assumption (see decision 01KN0YSCCQ93636R8CGA3BTW8B). With SMS now added, the following must change:

1. **UserPreferences interface**: Currently has only `email: EmailPreferences`. Needs an `sms: SmsPreferences` field (or a generic per-channel preferences structure).
2. **SmsPreferences type**: Needs to be created — at minimum: `enabled: boolean`, `subscribedCategories: NotificationCategory[]`. May not need frequency/digest settings since SMS is typically immediate.
3. **CreateUserPreferencesInput / UpdateUserPreferencesInput**: Need `sms?: Partial<SmsPreferences>` fields.
4. **getDefaultUserPreferences()**: Needs to return default SMS preferences (suggest: enabled=false by default, since SMS is opt-in unlike email).
5. **PreferencesService**: Needs a `shouldSendSms()` method analogous to `shouldSendEmail()`.
6. **preferences.validator.ts**: Needs a `shouldSendSms()` pure function analogous to `shouldSendEmail()`.
7. **NotificationService.sendNotification()**: Line 161 checks preferences only when `channel === 'email'`. Must be extended to check SMS preferences for `channel === 'sms'`.

The SMS adapter is fully functional for delivery, but without these preference changes, ALL SMS notifications will be sent unconditionally (no user opt-out). This is the same backward-compatible default as email when preferences aren't set, but it should be addressed before production rollout.","tags":["preferences","sms","multi-channel","breaking-assumption"],"scope":"src/models/user-preferences.ts","agent_id":"agent-8","id":"01KN0ZQJ7GAEMG69SVNR6XDT4M","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:41:53.264Z"}

postSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing
